name: pid-orphan-detector
question: Are there worker-shell-*.pid files referencing PIDs that are no longer alive?

schema.swept: array of {pid_file, referenced_pid, liveness_at_sweep, mtime} for orphan PIDs (deleted in --gc/--gc-cheap; reported but not deleted in default mode)
schema.alive: array of {pid_file, referenced_pid} for live-PID files (preserved)
schema.malformed: array of repo-relative paths to .pid files with non-integer content (NEVER deleted)
schema.briefing: string (one-line ORACLE BRIEFING summary; empty string when swept[] is empty)

_sweptEntry_schema.referenced_pid: integer (PID number read from file content)
_sweptEntry_schema.liveness_at_sweep: boolean (always false for entries in swept[]; alive PIDs are never swept)
_sweptEntry_schema.mtime: string (ISO-8601 UTC modification time at sweep evaluation)

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every (cheap mode in session-start hook); on-demand for full sweep

fallback: Read ltads/execution/ directly; for each worker-shell-*.pid file, read its content as a PID and probe liveness via `kill -0` (Unix) or `Get-Process -Id N -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` (Windows). Treat non-integer content as malformed (never delete). Before any delete, append a JSON line summarizing the entry to ltads/execution/_pid-summary.jsonl.

gcMode.summary: PID-orphan sweep (SWEEP-04). Sweeps ltads/execution/worker-shell-*.pid (and legacy worker-shell.pid) where the referenced PID is not alive. NO TTL per DEC-54: liveness is the sole criterion. Before each delete, appends a JSON entry to _pid-summary.jsonl preserving pid_file, referenced_pid, liveness_at_sweep:false, mtime, sweptAt (audit trail preserved per SWEEP-08).

cheap.behavior: Session-start path. If the per-file probe + summary write + delete loop exceeds 500ms wall-time, the sweep aborts cleanly and defers to the next session-start (graceful absence). 500ms (vs. dispatchable-sessions' 100ms) absorbs Windows powershell.exe cold-start probe cost (~80-200ms per call); see run.sh comment for rationale. Output is empty on success; warning on stderr on failure.
cheap.exit_codes: Always 0 (graceful). Internal failures are logged to stderr and never block the session-start briefing.

on_demand.behavior: Deeper scan with no time budget. Reports a single-line JSON summary on stdout: {swept, alive, malformed, briefing} (same shape as default mode but the swept[] entries' files have been deleted).

summary_schema.swept: array of {pid_file, referenced_pid, liveness_at_sweep:false, mtime} -- files deleted
summary_schema.malformed: array of repo-relative paths -- NEVER deleted

gcMode.criteria: File path under ltads/execution/, basename matches worker-shell-*.pid (or legacy worker-shell.pid), file content reads as positive integer, AND liveness probe returns 'not alive' for that PID. Malformed (non-integer) content is NEVER swept (failure-open). Alive PIDs are NEVER swept regardless of age.
gcMode.action: 1) Read PID number from file content. 2) Validate numeric (else -> malformed[]). 3) Probe liveness (alive -> alive[]). 4) Append JSON line to ltads/execution/_pid-summary.jsonl (atomic append). 5) Verify summary write succeeded (read-back last line). 6) Delete the .pid file. Summary write must succeed before delete; on failure the file is preserved.
gcMode.atomic_semantics: Per-file delete is sequential and uses POSIX-atomic append for _pid-summary.jsonl (each entry < PIPE_BUF / 4KB, atomic across concurrent appenders). No directory-level lock -- concurrent sweeps from different sessions are race-tolerant: each sweep reads files that exist at scan time; if two sweeps target the same file, the second one's delete becomes a no-op (idempotent). _pid-summary.jsonl may receive duplicate entries in rare double-sweep cases; audit trail favors over-recording.

provenance.note: Caps unbounded growth of ltads/execution/ from worker-shell-*.pid files left by crashed worker shells. Trigger incident: SessionHelm 2026-05-07 audit (10 orphan PIDs). Mirrors CSI-05's liveness probe and RETENTION-05's audit-trail invariant. NO TTL: liveness is binary, age is irrelevant (DEC-54). Three-leg defense (DEC-55): SessionEnd hook (proactive) + session-start --gc-cheap (reactive) + operator --gc (explicit), all sharing the same liveness probe via scripts/lib/pid-liveness.{sh,ps1}.
